cross_domain_targets.summary: Discovery-stage 2020 mayoral candidacy tying Kate Colin to the San Rafael general election and mayoral seat.
Outside-spending committee promoted from the public 2020 independent-expenditure folder.
Public Form 496 title showing SR Chamber of Commerce PAC supporting Kate Colin.

not_implied: The 2020 outside-money filing does not imply that Kate Colin's 2024 homelessness comments were caused by that earlier campaign support.
The public title-level filing row does not expose the expenditure amount or underlying donor chain.

cross_domain_targets.summary: Discovery-stage 2020 District 4 candidacy tying Rachel Kertz to the San Rafael general election and District 4 seat.
Outside-spending committee promoted from the public 2020 independent-expenditure folder.
Public Form 496 title showing WHINE PAC supporting Rachel Kertz.

not_implied: The 2020 outside-money filing does not imply that Rachel Kertz's 2024 statements about the Mahon Creek sanctioned camp were caused by that earlier campaign support.
The public title-level filing row does not expose the expenditure amount or vendor trail.

methodology_findings.summary: Public Form 496 titles are already strong enough to preserve spender committee, target candidate, and support direction even when raw filing artifacts remain unavailable.
The outside-money overlap layer can advance before amount-level donor or expenditure parsing, as long as the graph keeps the scope to actor, committee, filing, and target continuity.
Temporal distance matters. A later media quote can join to an earlier campaign-support record as identity continuity without implying causation.

notes: This worked example is intentionally narrower than a true donor or expenditure schedule analysis.
It uses city-side public folder-listing evidence plus previously accepted canonical actor joins.
